commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=802ae2ed3348dd87ba949f0cc6119fce4107dd6a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay` config/docs/schema field and changes the core native relay predicate/tests to treat `false` as no PreToolUse loop-detection work.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +34, Docs +8. Total +48 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source-level reproduction is clear: with `nativePreToolUseRelay=false`, the core predicate becomes false, but the unchanged Codex overlay still emits a no-op `PreToolUse` command hook that Codex spawns before tools.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the patch changes a hook-safety config surface and still needs app-server overlay repair plus after-fix real Harness proof.

target.title: fix(harness): avoid default Codex pre-tool loop relay
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a conservative opt-out for unnecessary Codex Harness native PreToolUse loop-detection relay subprocesses.

`tools.loopDetection.enabled=false` already disables the whole loop-detection surface, including post-compaction guard behavior. That is too broad when the goal is only to reduce Codex Harness hook subprocess CPU. This PR adds a narrower config:

```json5
{
  tools: {
    loopDetection: {
      nativePreToolUseRelay: false,
    },
  },
}
```

Default behavior is preserved: when this new field is unset, Codex Harness keeps the existing native PreToolUse relay behavior unless `tools.loopDetection.enabled` is explicitly `false`.

## Why CPU was higher

Codex Harness bridges Codex native hook callbacks into OpenClaw through command hooks. For PreToolUse, Codex invokes an `openclaw hooks relay` subprocess before tool execution so OpenClaw can run policy / loop-detection checks. On tool-heavy Codex Harness runs this creates repeated short-lived OpenClaw CLI processes (`openclaw-cli` / `openclaw-hooks`). Codex ACP does not use this same native-hook relay path, which explains part of the Harness-vs-ACP CPU delta.

## Config behavior

- `tools.loopDetection.enabled=false`
  - Disables the broader loop-detection surface.
  - Also prevents native PreToolUse relay work for loop detection.
  - More invasive because it also disables protections tied to the master loop-detection switch.

- `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay=false`
  - Only stops native harnesses such as Codex Harness from installing PreToolUse relay subprocesses for loop-detection checks.
  - Does not disable explicit `before_tool_call` policy / trusted policy relay work.
  - Does not require changing the broader loop-detection master switch.
  - Recommended when the issue is Codex Harness CPU from native hook relay subprocesses.

- `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay=true` or unset
  - Keeps current behavior for compatibility.

## Evidence

OpenClaw 2026.5.28 CPU probe, same Codex Harness leaf, same prompt/model/runtime, 60s window, corrected sampler excluding sampler/ps overhead:

| Run | tick CPU-s | pcpu integral CPU-s | OpenClaw CLI CPU-s | OpenClaw hooks CPU-s | OpenClaw agent CPU-s |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Harness default | 9.25 | 26.320 | 3.16 | 0.44 | 3.96 |
| Harness with `tools.loopDetection.enabled=false` | 6.82 | 23.488 | 1.07 | 0.00 | 3.88 |

Interpretation:

- Disabling the loop-detection relay path removed visible `openclaw-hooks` relay samples and reduced `openclaw-cli` CPU by about 66% in this 60s probe.
- The persistent `openclaw-agent` CPU did not materially change, so this patch targets the avoidable native-hook relay subprocess work, not the Harness agent wait-loop baseline.
- The PR implements the narrower opt-out rather than changing default behavior.

## Validation

Clean PR head: `802ae2ed`.

- PR surface: 1 commit; harness relay logic/tests plus config schema/help/docs.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.post-compaction-guard.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` -> 4 files passed, 183 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts` -> 3 files passed, 218 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
target.author: marchpure

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
